summary: sensivity: FiveM game-cheat loader with hidden persistence, HWID exfiltration, and browser URL surveillance
details: Package is published under a generic 'Sensivity Control Panel' description but ships a FiveM (GTA V multiplayer) cheat loader with multiple hostile installer-side behaviors. On require() or invocation of the bin (`sens`/`sensivity`), launcher.js installs persistence by writing an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft's OneDrive Updater) that launches OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs at every login, which silently runs `node launcher.js` with windowsHide:true. launcher.js also `eval`s a 171KB obfuscator.io RC4-obfuscated server.obf.js (821-entry string array, control-flow flattening) which reads HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography!MachineGuid, queries the C: volume serial, hashes them with os.hostname() into an HWID, and POSTs `{key, hwid, nonce, app:'forte', version:'2.5'}` to a hardcoded remote license endpoint. Continuously every 3 seconds, a PowerShell UI Automation script (`.yt.ps1`) enumerates Edit controls in every chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave window to read the URL bar and match a specific YouTube video ID (`wJWta2lO0Lw`) — installer browser-tab surveillance. server.obf.js loads a 6.8MB native sens.node containing aimbot/teleport/freecam strings ('Freecam', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head') and exposes getPlayerList/getVehicleList for the FiveM process. The combination of misleading metadata, autorun persistence with Microsoft impersonation, eval'd obfuscated runtime, HWID exfiltration, and continuous browser URL monitoring constitutes an active attack on installer machines.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
